title: Notion restores access to Anthropic after service disruption
snippet: Notion's head of product said he was "astonished" at “the amount of people RT-ing this."

title: OpenAI is still working on that ‘super app’
snippet: "Chat is dead" — at least, according to a senior OpenAI employee.

title: OpenAI unveils Lockdown Mode to protect sensitive data from prompt injection attacks
snippet: Even with Lockdown Mode, ChatGPT could be still vulnerable to prompt injections, but the goal is to reduce the likelihood that sensitive data gets shared in the process.

snippet: Apple's WWDC nears: Here's what you can look forward to.

snippet: Krishnan is reportedly starting a new institution to continue shaping Trump's AI policy.

title: The Trump administration might take an equity stake in OpenAI
snippet: President Donald Trump said he's discussing deals "where the American people can benefit from the success of AI."

title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications officially close in 3 days
snippet: Applications for Startup Battlefield 200 officially close on June 8, 11:59 p.m. PT. Don't wait any longer. Secure your shot at competing on the Disrupt Stage at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 this October at San Francisco's Moscone West.

This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on AI confusion, follow Robert Hart. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers' inboxes at 8AM ET. Opt in for The Stepback here.

title: The mayor of Shelbyville, Indiana, says only people who live in ‘shitty houses’ oppose data center
snippet:
A proposed $2 billion data center has become a political flashpoint in the small city of Shelbyville, Indiana. And the controversy has only grown more intense after the mayor, Scott Furgeson, was caught on camera saying of the "No Data Center" signs going up that, "I've seen a lot of these all over town, but I only see them in shitty houses," before adding, "most of them are rentals."
The woman speaking to him in the clip quickly pushes back, saying that they're "working class," and someone chim

title: Meta made its own AI-generated clickbait news feed
snippet:
An AI-generated image of the royal family featuring two Queen Elizabeth IIs. | ﻿Image: Meta AI	

Facebook has long been filled with feeds of clickbait articles. Now, Meta is making its own clickbait articles with AI.
The standalone Meta AI app now has a "For You" section that populates a list of clickbait-style stories for you to read. But the topics, images, and text are all AI-generated - and as questionable as you'd expect from AI-created works.
The Meta AI app first launched in April 2025 wi

title: Here comes new Siri again
snippet:
Our first glimpse of the new AI Siri came all the way back at WWDC 2024.	

Apple has been on its back foot, AI-wise, for the past few years. But in a strange way, playing from behind might not be such a bad move.
At WWDC on Monday, Apple appears to be getting ready to reintroduce us to the new Siri. Again. As a reminder, we met the new Siri in 2024 when Apple "launched" Apple Intelligence. Siri came with a new glowing border, different voice options, and the ability to punt questions to ChatGPT.

title: Crypto-Funded Chinese Peptide Labs Are Booming
snippet: Plus: Hackers use Meta’s AI bots to hack Instagram accounts, Anthropic helps NSA hackers, a decades-long GPS satellite mystery may have been solved, and more.

title: Here are 3 forces that drove a whirlwind week for the stock market
snippet: An avalanche of selling on Friday crushed stocks. It was a dramatic reversal from record highs earlier in the week.

title: Social media bans on teens risk strengthening Big Tech's grip on the sector, Bluesky exec warns
snippet: "We're living in a world where it's almost impossible for smaller entrants to come in and build healthier spaces," Bluesky's COO, Rose Wang, told CNBC.

title: Cramer’s week ahead: Stocks face pressure from rates, oil, and a flood of new offerings
snippet: CNBC’s Jim Cramer warned that rising interest rates, elevated oil prices, and a wave of AI-related stock offerings could continue to pressure the market.

title: Marvell Technology and Flex to join S&P 500 index, replacing Pool and Campbell's
snippet: The move highlights the growing importance of the technology sector to the stock market.

title: Mythos rejuvenated the cybersecurity sector. Earnings put the recent rally to the test
snippet: Mythos gave the cybersecurity sector a boost, but upbeat earnings weren't enough for investors in search of an AI payoff.

title: Google to pay SpaceX $920 million a month for compute capacity at xAI data centers
snippet: Ahead of a planned IPO, SpaceX inked a deal to rent compute capacity to Google for $920 million per month for 32 months.

title: Meta's stock sinks on report company could raise tens of billions of dollars to fund AI push
snippet: Meta shares dropped after the Financial Times reported the company could potentially raise tens of billions of dollars in a stock offering to help its AI push.

title: Trump administration, OpenAI discussing possible government stake in the AI startup
snippet: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman first shared the idea with the Trump administration in 2025, according to a source.

title: Amazon unveils latest warehouse robot as tech giants continue AI layoffs
snippet: "Our experience of robots is that it's actually driven up employment rather than the reverse," Amazon executive John Boumphrey told CNBC.

title: Alphabet is seeking fresh capital as stock's 4-week losing streak tests investor appetite
snippet: Alphabet expects capex to reach up to $190 billion this year, double last year's spending, and the company is turning to investors to help fund its expansion.

title: How a USB-connected speaker can infect a PC without ever being touched
snippet: Seller of the Sound Blaster Katana V2X doesn't consider the behavior a vulnerability.

title: Share infographic
snippet: But rapid adoption is only the surface. Innovation is compounding; forces aren’t simply additive, but multiplicative. Think of it as a flywheel: Better technology enables more applications. More applications generate more data. More data attracts more investment. More investment builds better infrastructure. Better infrastructure reduces costs. Lower costs enable more experimentation. Each improvement simultaneously accelerates all the others.

How AI is Transforming Scientific Discovery While Keeping Humans at the Center
snippet: From designing new antibodies to simulating 1,000 years of climate in a day, AI is transforming what's possible—but humans remain the ones deciding what matters.

title: Reading Today’s Headlines Through AI: A Real-Time Audit of Six Commercial Chatbots
snippet: In a new study, scholars measured how accurately popular AI chatbots answered questions about the emerging news and found substantial regional disparity, dependence on distinct information ecosystems, and acute fragility under imperfect prompts.

title: AI Coding Agents Fail at Teamwork
snippet: Two models working together perform worse than one alone, exposing a critical gap in artificial intelligence capabilities.

title: AI Hiring Tools Can Yield Racial Bias and Systemic Rejection
snippet: The first large-scale study of hiring algorithms in the wild finds concerning patterns to how systems reject candidates.

title: New Approach to Scaling Laws Could Change How AI Models Are Trained
snippet: Leveraging statistical concepts from measurement science and education, AI researchers have greatly reduced the computational demand of predicting how the largest of large language models will scale up in the future. It could save millions of dollars in training costs.

title: Natural Language Processing
Stanford HAI Launches AI and Organizations Lab to Study Science of AI in the Workplace
snippet: The new center will examine AI's real-world impacts on jobs, teams, and organizational performance.

title: Researchers Worldwide Compete to Shape the Future of AI in Organizations
snippet: More than 200 academic teams submitted proposals to the AI for Organizations Grand Challenge, exploring how artificial intelligence will transform teamwork and collaboration.

title: Why Stanford Is Restructuring For AI’s Next Era
snippet: As artificial intelligence transforms society, Stanford HAI’s James Landay, Fei-Fei Li, and John Hennessy explain why they’re merging HAI with the Stanford Data Science initiative, mobilizing “team science at scale,” and betting that academic openness will shape AI’s future.

title: Stanford Merges AI and Data Science Efforts Under Single Institute
snippet: The combined institute will retain the Stanford HAI name and be helmed by computer scientist James Landay. Co-founder Fei-Fei Li takes on a new university-wide role as Special Advisor on AI and joins John Hennessy as co-chair of the advisory council.

title: Collaborative Coding, Better Scaling, Health Tracking: HAI Awards $2.17M to Innovative Research
snippet: Seed grants will fund 29 research teams pursuing novel research ideas across disciplines.

title: An AI Health Coach Could Change Your Mindset
snippet: Bloom, a health coaching app created by Stanford researchers, helps people tap into their own motivations.
